intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Tractema pumila. However, I can provide you with information on a plant that shares a similar name, but with a more recognized botanical name - Trachetum pumilum, which is now known as Thalictrum minus. 

Trachetum pumilum, or Meadow Rue (Thalictrum minus), is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Meadow Rue include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Meadow Rue include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
